examples.title: LR Parsing Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode for the LR parsing algorithm
examples.code:
INPUT: An input string w and an LR parsing table
OUTPUT: If w is in L(G), a bottom-up parse; otherwise, an error

INITIALIZE:
 Push state s0 onto the stack
 Set ip (input pointer) to the first symbol of w$

REPEAT:
 Let s be the state on top of the stack
 Let a be the symbol pointed to by ip
 IF ACTION[s, a] = shift s' THEN
 Push a and then s' onto the stack
 Advance ip to the next input symbol
 ELSE IF ACTION[s, a] = reduce A -> beta THEN
 Pop 2 * |beta| symbols from the stack
 Let s' be the state now on top of the stack
 Push A and then GOTO[s', A] onto the stack
 Output the production A -> beta
 ELSE IF ACTION[s, a] = accept THEN
 Return success (parsing complete)
 ELSE
 Call error-recovery routine
examples.explanation: This pseudocode describes the LR parsing algorithm, which uses a stack and a two-part parsing table (ACTION and GOTO) to perform shift and reduce actions.

examples.title: Python Implementation of LR Parsing Algorithm
examples.description: A Python implementation of the LR parsing algorithm
examples.explanation: This Python implementation of the LR parsing algorithm uses a dictionary to represent the parsing table and a list to represent the stack.

examples.title: C Implementation of LR Parsing Algorithm
examples.description: A C implementation of the LR parsing algorithm
examples.explanation: This C implementation of the LR parsing algorithm uses a struct to represent stack elements and an array to represent the stack.
